content: I'm working on my kthread abstraction, one thing occurs to me is that I used to name the new thread function as try_new because back then our Box and Arc implementations all used try_new(), but seems to me now we switch to a naming scheme that new() can return an Result? So I think I should use the name new then?
(It was one of the advantages of having our own alloc)
So essentially, if there is e.g. a fallible and a infallible version of a method, then you probably want to use try_ to indicate that; otherwise you can avoid it.
Now here's the $100 question: should we document this somewhere? ;-)
I would say they are coding guidelines, since we would want those writing patches to know about that (of course, reviewers also apply the coding ones).
